message.content:
If you want tools that are affordable but still feel solid, durable, and pleasant to use, these brands consistently hit that sweet spot:

• Ryobi – Probably the best value-for-money cordless tool system. Good performance, huge lineup, very wallet‑friendly.  
• Craftsman – Solid, dependable, and inexpensive. Great for homeowners and light‑to‑medium DIY work.  
• Kobalt – Lowe’s house brand; surprisingly robust and well‑built for the price.  
• Ridgid – Often a step up in build quality while still affordable, plus excellent warranties.  
• Skil (especially the PWRCore 20 line) – Smooth, ergonomic tools with good battery tech at low prices.

If you want the simplest recommendation: pick Ryobi for the biggest ecosystem or Ridgid if you want tougher tools without jumping to pro-tier prices.
